qa_pair.question: Open a headless browser and navigate to the URL `data:text/html,ABC`. How many anchor URLs does browser_get_links report for the page?

Navigate to `data:text/html,

Hi

`. Inspect the element matching the CSS selector `#t` with the full field preset and report its outer_html value exactly.
Navigate to `data:text/html,`. List the frames, then evaluate the JavaScript expression `document.body.textContent` inside the child frame (not the main frame). What string is returned?

Navigate to `data:text/html,<input id="i" value="old">`. Using a key sequence on `#i`, hold Ctrl and press `a` to select all, then type the text `new`. Then evaluate `document.getElementById('i').value`. What is the value?

Navigate to `data:text/html,
x
`. Use browser_set_value with mode `text` to set `#d` to `filled`, then evaluate `document.getElementById('d').textContent`. What is the result?
Navigate to `data:text/html,<input id="i">`. Use browser_set_value (default mode) to set `#i` to `hello`, then evaluate `document.getElementById('i').value`. What is the value?

Navigate to https://example.com and export the page to PDF with browser_pdf (no save_path, so the bytes come back base64-encoded). Decode the bytes and report the first four characters (the file-format signature).

Navigate to `data:text/html,hi

body

` and capture an MHTML archive with browser_save_mhtml (no save_path). Decode the base64 bytes; what are the first five characters of the MHTML archive?

Navigate to `data:text/html,
ready
` and call browser_wait_for_load with ready_state `complete`. Afterward, evaluate `document.readyState`. What value does it return?
Open a headless browser, set the window with browser_set_window using mode `size`, width 1024 and height 768. Then read it back with browser_get_window. What width does it report?
